Logging functions for pipeline execution

Source: R/Utilities.R
clog.Rd

A comprehensive set of logging functions for tracking pipeline execution with different log levels and message types. Supports timestamped messages with various prefixes for different purposes.

Usage

clog(message = "", char = ">>>", level = "INFO")

clog_start()

clog_end()

clog_SS(message)

clog_MS(message)

clog_step(message)

clog_loop(message)

clog_args(message)

clog_normal(message)

clog_title(message)

clog_warn(message)

clog_error(message, call. = TRUE)

Arguments

message

Character, the log message to display

char

Character, prefix character(s) for the log message (default: ">>>")

level

Character, log level - one of "DEBUG", "INFO", "WARNING", "ERROR", "CRITICAL"

call.

Logical, whether to include call information in error messages (default: TRUE)

Value

NULL (invisible), messages are printed to console

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
clog("This is an info message")
clog_start()
clog_step("Starting analysis step")
clog_loop("Processing sample 1 of 10")
clog_normal("Normal operation message")
clog_warn("This is a warning")
clog_end()
} # }
